@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flux-710-notifications-information-content-and-filtering-of-info-alerts",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flux: Notifications - information content and filtering of info alerts",
+    "description": "Notifications - information content and filtering of info alerts. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current flux deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your flux version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux\nhelm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working flux installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review flux configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant flux configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux\nkubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux\n```\nHi, \nI am currently working on the migration of our flux(v1)/helm-operator setups to flux(v2)/Toolkit.\n\nregarding notifications i am struggling to get a reasonable configuration regarding of information and volume."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Notifications - information content and filtering of info…",
+        "description": "I don't think so,  want's to filter the info notifications not Kubernetes events.\n\nThe controller-name shows up as the username, see https://toolkit.fluxcd.io/_files/slack-info-alert.png\n\nThis is a bug tracked in https://github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/issues/236\n\nFlux1 GC is very different to Flux2 GC. The v2 GC tracks objects without querying the Kubernetes API for each Kind, this means GC can be run under a restricted service account with no cluster wide access (unlike v1 that needs a\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux\nkubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Notifications - information content and filtering of info…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I don't think so,  want's to filter the info notifications not Kubernetes events.\n\nThe controller-name shows up as the username, see https://toolkit.fluxcd.io/_files/slack-info-alert.png\n\nThis is a bug tracked in https://github.com/fluxcd/kustomize-controller/issues/236\n\nFlux1 GC is very different to Flux2 GC.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "flux",
+      "graduated",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "flux"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Namespace",
+      "Role",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/710",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 6,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-01T06:36:25.346Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flux-710-notifications-information-content-and-filtering-of-info-alerts",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flux: Notifications - information content and filtering of info alerts",
+    "description": "Notifications - information content and filtering of info alerts. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current flux deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your flux version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux\nhelm list -n flux 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working flux installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review flux configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant flux configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux\nkubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux\n```\nHi, \nI am currently working on the migration of our flux(v1)/helm-operator setups to flux(v2)/Toolkit.\n\nregarding notifications i am struggling to get a reasonable configuration regarding of information and volume."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Notifications - information content and filtering of info",
+        "description": "> One thing that would help here is my suggestion in fluxcd/pkg#56 (comment)\n\nI don't think so,  want's to filter the info notifications not Kubernetes events.\n\n> Also currently the alerts show no information of the kind of toolkit object (or controller)\n\nThe controller-name shows up as the username, see https://toolkit.fluxcd.io/_files/slack-info-alert.png\n\n> I also get alerts for every positive health check, but I have not found a way to filter certain alerts.\n\nThis is a bug tracked in\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux\nkubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Notifications - information content and filtering of info\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> One thing that would help here is my suggestion in fluxcd/pkg#56 (comment)\n\nI don't think so,  want's to filter the info notifications not Kubernetes events.",

+    }
+  },
